evals.prompt: Write a timely operator that takes a stream of (String, u64) and outputs the string repeated n times, where n is the u64. Use flat_map.
evals.expected_output: A simple flat_map or unary operator using Pipeline pact, no frontier needed

evals.prompt: Write a custom timely operator using unary_frontier that receives (String, i64) pairs and, once a timestamp is complete, emits the sum per key as (String, i64). Exchange by the string key.
evals.expected_output: A unary_frontier operator with Exchange pact, stash/emit/retain pattern, frontier check before emitting

assertions.text: Uses Exchange pact keyed on the string
Checks frontier before emitting (!frontier.less_equal or equivalent)
Drops capabilities after emitting (stash.retain or equivalent cleanup)

evals.prompt: Write a timely operator that receives u64 values and for each value x emits all numbers 0..x. It should yield after producing 10,000 records per scheduling to avoid flooding memory.
evals.expected_output: An operator using build_reschedule or Activator pattern, with a fuel/budget mechanism to limit output per scheduling

assertions.text: Uses a yielding mechanism (build_reschedule, Activator, or feedback loop)
Has a fuel/budget mechanism limiting output per scheduling (~10,000 records)
